@graph.description: This experiment automates the deployment, verification, and measurement of an IPv6 topology where a client has two paths to a destination server. The setup is intended for multipath transport protocol evaluation, such as MPTCP and MPQUIC, where a multihomed client is capable of using several networks simultaneously (e.g., a smartphone using both Wi-Fi and cellular). The topology is based on the testbed setup described by Rabitsch et al. (2018).
@graph.name: Topology for Multipath Experiments

@graph.description: The pos Framework: A Methodology and Toolchain for Reproducible Network Experiments
@graph.name: Plain Orchestrating Service

narva-install-dependencies
@graph.description: Installs common node-specific dependencies for traffic generation with iperf, packet captures with tshark, and data visualization with Python and matplotlib.

@graph.name: klaipeda-install-dependencies
@graph.description: Installs common node-specific dependencies for traffic generation with iperf, packet captures with tshark, and data visualization with Python and matplotlib.

@graph.name: klaipeda-topology
@graph.description: Configures the client by assigning one address per physical path and installing policy-routing rules so that each source address uses its intended path.

@graph.name: narva-topology
@graph.description: Configures the server by bridging two physical links that share a server address. The fast path to the client is configured with a fixed RTT and bandwidth; only the loss of the fast path varies.

@graph.name: klaipeda-run-client
@graph.description: Selects one of the paths available to the client, applies the configured shaping parameters, and connects to the server. It uses ping to assess whether the RTT was set correctly, while the iperf benchmark shows whether the configured rate limit is reachable.

@graph.name: klaipeda-run-client_run00
@graph.description: Each iteration configures one client-side path, validates the RTT with ping, and runs the iperf measurement towards the server to record the achievable throughput for the selected parameter combination.

@graph.name: data_run00
@graph.description: Contains raw data extracted from per-run .pcapng captures, storing the timestamp relative to the first observe packet and captured frame length.

@graph.name: narva-run-server
@graph.description: Starts a server bound to two Layer-4 paths and awaits incoming TCP connections from the client. It applies the corresponding rate, delay, and loss properties with tc, starts the tshark capture, and stores per-run artifacts for later analysis.

@graph.name: narva-run-server_run00
@graph.description: Each iteration configures the server-side network conditions for the selected parameter combination, starts the server endpoint, captures the traffic with tshark, and stores the per-run artifacts for later evaluation.

@graph.description: Each iteration configures one client-side path, validates the RTT with ping, and runs the iperf measurement towards the server to record the achievable throughput for the selected parameter combination.

throughput_heatmap.pdf
@graph.description: Provides a visual overview of all loop run combinations. Fast path throughput diminishes as loss increases; on the slow path, throughput gets smaller as the RTT, loss, and bandwidth change.

@graph.name: generate-throughput-heatmap
@graph.description: Visualizes the achieved throughput for each loop run combination in the form of a heatmap.
